title: Confirm an owned-account transfer
preconditions: Both transactions exist on distinct owned accounts; an agent or the owner has selected the proposed pair; automatic matching remains outside LEDGER.
postconditions: One active valid Transfer Pair is inspectable; each leg participates in that transfer only, both bank facts remain present, and the principal contributes zero External Spend. Later void, supersession, or revocation retires the pair atomically and ordinary unlinked treatment resumes until explicit valid replacement.
main_flow: 1. The agent retrieves both source transactions and supplies an Idempotency Identity. 2. The owner or agent explicitly requests a transfer link. 3. Tally validates distinct owned accounts, same currency, equal-and-opposite economic movements, active lifecycle state, and that neither leg participates in any active transfer or refund; supplied dates may differ. 4. Tally records one attributable transfer in which each leg has exactly one active transfer role, without changing either source transaction. 5. External Spend excludes the confirmed principal while account queries retain both legs. 6. Tally returns the relationship and zero-spend effect.
alt_flows: 3a. Transactions are on the same account, use different currencies, have the same sign, or principal magnitudes are not equal -> reject the link
3b. Either leg participates in any active transfer or refund relationship -> reject with a stable role conflict
3c. Only one leg exists -> leave it unconfirmed and counted under the ordinary spend policy
3d. A transfer fee is embedded in one bank leg rather than a separate Canonical Transaction -> leave the pair unconfirmed in the first release
4a. The relationship write fails -> neither leg is partially linked
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"Two canonical transactions appear to be the legs of a transfer between accounts owned by the owner.","status":"draft"}
